title: Lilith in human history
wikitext:
[]: Lilith in human history             Quote Post by anti_pope » Sun Dec 10, 2017 2:14 pm So, I hope there are still enough people around here for a little discussion about that. What I asked myself is, what did Lilith in the history of humanity? Did she just sleep in the Black Moon or did she wander around on the surface? And when she was in the Black Moon, do you think someone found her before SEELE and GEHIRN did? The Black Moon is like a huge cave under ground and maybe there was a crack in the "hull" and someone got in and found Lilith. That's a topic I'm really interested in and maybe an interesting discussion get's started ^^

[]: Re: Lilith in human history             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Sun Dec 10, 2017 3:53 pm I would like to think that Lilith walked around at first and in doing so s/he influenced ancient mythology as in it there are many references to Star people in some mythologies that would be very fitting to Lilith in that she'd be part of the mythology but humanity not really realizing it. http://www.collective-evolution.com/2017/01/02/the-star-people-extraterrestrials-from-a-native-american-first-nations-perspective/

[]: Re: Lilith in human history             Quote Post by Cybermat47 » Mon Dec 11, 2017 5:04 am I always just thought that she landed and stayed in her egg as she created life. The idea of her guiding early humanity is certainly an interesting one, though.

[]: Re: Lilith in human history             Quote Post by Bhorium » Mon Dec 11, 2017 6:20 am silvermoonlight wrote: I would like to think that Lilith walked around at first and in doing so s/he influenced ancient mythology as in it there are many references to Star people in some mythologies that would be very fitting to Lilith in that she'd be part of the mythology but humanity not really realizing it. http://www.collective-evolution.com/2017/01/02/the-star-people-extraterrestrials-from-a-native-american-first-nations-perspective/

[]: Re: Lilith in human history             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Mon Dec 11, 2017 6:34 am Hate to sound like a stick in the mud, but I highly doubt Lilith wandered around the Earth's surface, personally. I mean, she landed on Earth so long ago, there would be no way of people finding out about her arrival from the stars unless she telepathically communicated with them. And, if that were the case, and she were interested in influencing her descendants like that, I figure her ATF'd be powerful enough she could do that from a distance, without leaving her physical location. Anyway, Mother goddesses can come from elsewhere. Though I do like the topic: This is definitely an interesting line of thought. Thanks, OP!

[]: Re: Lilith in human history             Quote Post by anti_pope » Tue Dec 12, 2017 4:18 am Cybermat47 wrote: I always just thought that she landed and stayed in her egg as she created life. The idea of her guiding early humanity is certainly an interesting one, though.
